spec_match.rationale: The page now uses a server route for real Brotli and real dictionary-compressed byte counts, but labels the dictionary payload as a local stand-in rather than claiming to emit a byte-for-byte Shared Brotli/Zstandard stream.

interactive.rationale: Editable dictionary and content textareas plus a hash-mismatch toggle drive live server measurements and header output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and concept lede now describe measured Brotli, a measured dictionary-compressed payload, dcb framing overhead, and the explicit encoder caveat.

edge_cases.rationale: The hash-mismatch toggle demonstrates the Available-Dictionary SHA-256 validation failure path and the regular Brotli fallback.

openQuestions.title: Swap the stand-in payload for a true dcb/dcz encoder when available
openQuestions.detail: The route no longer uses arithmetic: it measures real regular Brotli and a real zlib dictionary-compressed payload, then adds the RFC 9842 dcb header size. It still cannot emit a byte-for-byte Shared Brotli or Zstandard dictionary stream because native JavaScript and Node-compatible zlib do not expose that encoder. If a maintained encoder is added later, replace the proof payload with exact dcb/dcz bytes.

summary: Resolved the major measurement issue and both moderate gaps: the page now calls a server route for measured compression sizes, includes an explicit measured/stand-in disclaimer, updates the feature blurb, and provides a SHA-256 mismatch scenario that falls back to regular Brotli.
